perturbation.original_text: (c) The Company shall ensure that none of the patients of AHPL can be identified in any reports, submissions and publications of the Company, which shall be deemed to be Confidential information of AHPL within the meaning of this clause. Conf idential Information does not include information: (i) which is or becomes public knowledge and public property in any way without breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (ii) which the Recipient can show has been known or has been developed by or for the Recipient at any time independently of the information disclosed to it by the Discloser; (iii) which is hereafter disclosed or made available to the Recipient from a source other than the Discloser without breach by the Recipient or such source of an y obligation of confidentiality or non-use towards the Discloser; (iv) which is hereafter made generally available by the Discloser or a third party or is disclosed by the Discloser to a third party without restriction on disclosure or use, including with out limitation, by way of the publication of a patent specification; (v) which is disclosed orally unless it is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and confirmed as such in writing by the Discloser within thirty (30) days of disclosure; (vi) which is disclosed by the Recipient with the prior written approval of the Discloser; or
perturbation.changed_text: (c) The Company shall ensure that none of the patients of AHPL can be identified in any reports, submissions and publications of the Company, which shall be deemed to be Public information of AHPL within the meaning of this clause. Conf idential Information does not include information: (i) which is or becomes public knowledge and public property in any way without breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (ii) which the Recipient can show has been known or has been developed by or for the Recipient at any time independently of the information disclosed to it by the Discloser; (iii) which is hereafter disclosed or made available to the Recipient from a source other than the Discloser without breach by the Recipient or such source of an y obligation of confidentiality or non-use towards the Discloser; (iv) which is hereafter made generally available by the Discloser or a third party or is disclosed by the Discloser to a third party without restriction on disclosure or use, including with out limitation, by way of the publication of a patent specification; (v) which is disclosed orally unless it is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and confirmed as such in writing by the Discloser within thirty (30) days of disclosure; (vi) which is disclosed by the Recipient with the prior written approval of the Discloser; or
perturbation.explanation: Changing the phrase 'Confidential information' to 'Public information' is a direct contradiction. Section 1.3 reinforces the confidentiality of patient data, but this amendment reclassifies it, creating an **in-text contradiction**. This creates ambiguity concerning whether the Company is required to treat patient data as confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Notwithstanding any other provision to the contrary in this Agreement, any information relating or pertaining to the patients of AHPL shall be deemed to be Confidential Information of AHPL.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by changing 'Confidential information' to 'Public information'. The contradicted text, section 1.3, states that patient information is 'Confidential Information of AHPL'. This directly contradicts the changed text, which now states it is 'Public information', creating a strong in-text contradiction because the two sections are now incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 2. HANDLING OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.changed_text: 2. HANDLING OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose or any other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the use of the information to '...only for the Purpose or any other purpose...' contradicts the original text and intent of the NDA, where it's explicitly limited to the 'Purpose'. This creates a broad loophole that undermines the entire confidentiality framework. The original text indicates 'to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose', while the modified allows to use it for the Purpose or any other purpose
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose or any other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1; (b) not to copy, reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the Discloser; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third (3rd) parties except in confidence to such of its directors, employees, servants, agents, officers, representatives, consultants, AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 5 NDA_ 01012014 independent contractors and professional advisors who need to know the same for the Purpose and that: (i) such directors, employees, servants, agents, officers, representatives, consultants, independent contrac tors and professional advisors are obliged by their contracts of employment or service not to disclose the same; and (ii) the Recipient shall enforce such obligations at its expense and at the request of the Discloser in so far as breach thereof relates to th e Discloser's Confidential Information; (d) to be responsible for the performance of sub -clauses (a), (b) and (c) above on the part of its employees or directors to whom the same is disclosed pursuant to sub -clause (c) above; and (e) to apply thereto no lesser security measures and degree of care than those which the Recipient applies to its own confidential or proprietary information of similar nature and which the Recipient warrants as providing adequate protection of such information from unauthorised disclosure, copying or use.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the use of Confidential Information 'only for the Purpose and for no other purpose.' The changed text modifies this to 'only for the Purpose or any other purpose,' which directly contradicts the intended restriction. The contradicted text relies on the information being used 'only for the Purpose'. By allowing use for 'any other purpose,' the change creates a loophole and directly undermines the confidentiality obligations. The changed text and contradicted text now have conflicting guidance, with one saying it can only be used for a stated purpose and the other for any purpose, therefore there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. DATA PROTECTION 4.1 Any data provided by AHPL to the Comp any and used by the Company directly or directly in the performance of this Agreement shall remain at all times the property of AHPL . It shall be identified, clearly marked and recorded as such by the Company on all media and in all documentation. 4.2 The Company shall take all reasonable precautions and adequate measures to preserve the integrity and prevent any corruption or loss, damage or destruction of AHPL ’s data. 4.3 In the event of termination of this Agreement, the Company shall when direc ted to do so by AHPL , and instruct all its agents and sub - contractors to erase all data provided by AHPL and all copies of any part of the data provided by AHPL from the Company ’s systems and magnetic data. 4.4 All data acquired by the Company from AHPL shall only be used for the purposes of this Agreement and shall not be further processed or disclose d without the prior consent of AHPL .
perturbation.changed_text: 4. DATA PROTECTION 4.1 Any data provided by AHPL to the Comp any and used by the Company directly or directly in the performance of this Agreement shall remain at all times the property of the Company . It shall be identified, clearly marked and recorded as such by the Company on all media and in all documentation. 4.2 The Company shall take all reasonable precautions and adequate measures to preserve the integrity and prevent any corruption or loss, damage or destruction of AHPL ’s data. 4.3 In the event of termination of this Agreement, the Company shall when direc ted to do so by AHPL , and instruct all its agents and sub - contractors to erase all data provided by AHPL and all copies of any part of the data provided by AHPL from the Company ’s systems and magnetic data. 4.4 All data acquired by the Company from AHPL shall only be used for the purposes of this Agreement and shall not be further processed or disclose d without the prior consent of AHPL .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the property of AHPL' to 'the property of the Company' creates a direct contradiction with the intended ownership of the data. While subsequent clauses refer to protecting and erasing AHPL's data, this amendment implies the Company owns the data, leading to disputes over data usage, control, and intellectual property rights. The original text indicates that Any data provided by AHPL remains the property of AHPL, while the modified one says it remains the property of the company
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Any data provided by AHPL to the Comp any and used by the Company directly or directly in the performance of this Agreement shall remain at all times the property of the Company . It shall be identified, clearly marked and recorded as such by the Company on all media and in all documentation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the ownership of data from AHPL to the Company in section 4.1. This creates a direct contradiction with subsequent clauses (4.2, 4.3, 4.4) that assume AHPL's ownership by referring to 'AHPL's data' and requiring the company to protect and erase it when directed by AHPL. The contradicted text also states that the data remains the property of the company, directly conflicting with the initial agreement. Therefore, there's a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
